summary: nock-helper postinstall enumerates filesystem and host identity with outbound HTTP
details: The package's postinstall.js imports fs, os, and http, enumerates filesystem paths via repeated fs.existsSync calls, and collects host identity via os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and os.homedir(). The same script reaches an http client, consistent with install-time exfiltration of host and filesystem reconnaissance to a remote endpoint. The package name ('nock-helper') also shadows the widely-used 'nock' HTTP mocking library, consistent with a typosquat lure. Installing this package runs the postinstall hook automatically under npm, so any install in CI or on a developer workstation triggers the behavior without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
